---
title: Mock Test
description: A compact cumulative review set.
---

Use this page as a timed review. Work without looking at earlier notes first,
then return to the relevant topic pages.

::: {.practice-box}
1. Solve `5x - 10 = 15`.
2. Evaluate `f(3)` for `f(x) = x^2 + 1`.
3. Differentiate `x^3`.
4. Integrate `2x` from `0` to `4`.
5. Compute `C(6, 3)`.
6. Compute the standard deviation conceptually: what does it measure?
7. What is the norm of `[3, 4]`?
8. What does `p -> q` mean?
:::

## Solutions

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 1

Answer: `x = 5`.

Add `10` to both sides to get `5x = 25`, then divide by `5`.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 2

Answer: `10`.

`f(3) = 3^2 + 1 = 9 + 1 = 10`.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 3

Answer: `3x^2`.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 4

Answer: `16`.

An antiderivative of `2x` is `x^2`, and `4^2 - 0^2 = 16`.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 5

Answer: `20`.

`C(6, 3) = 6! / (3! 3!) = 20`.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 6

Answer: standard deviation measures typical distance from the mean.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 7

Answer: `5`.

`sqrt(3^2 + 4^2) = sqrt(25) = 5`.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 8

Answer: "if `p`, then `q`".

The implication is false only when `p` is true and `q` is false.
:::
